title: Memory Management in Agentic Systems Part 3: Cross-Session Memory with mem0, LangMem & Semantic Caching
summary: Complete the memory journey by adding cross-session semantic memory using mem0 with Google ADK and LangMem with LangChain. Learn how embeddings enable semantic matching, how LangCache eliminates redundant LLM calls, and how to wire an async full-stack pipeline that knows your users across every session.

title: Memory Management in Agentic Systems Part 2: Short-Term & Long-Term Memory with Redis
summary: Upgrade your agent's memory with a two-layer Redis plus SQL architecture. Learn rolling windows and running summaries for active sessions, cold-resume for returning users, and async Redis with adk-redis (Google ADK) and redis-py (LangChain).

title: Memory Management in Agentic Systems Part 1: Session Memory with SQLite
summary: Give your AI agent its first memory using SQLite. Build a customer support bot that stores and retrieves conversation history, design a sessions and messages schema, then upgrade from blocking sync I/O to async aiosqlite for concurrent users.

title: RL Agents Part 6: RLAIF, Reward Hacking, and Credit Assignment
summary: Final part of the RL agents series covering RLAIF-based critics, reward hacking failure patterns and mitigations, KL regularization, and why long-horizon credit assignment remains the hardest open problem.

title: RL Agents Part 5: Online Learning from Agent Trajectories
summary: A practical guide to closed-loop agent improvement with live trajectory logging, replay buffers, STaR-style updates, anti-collapse safeguards, and diversity-aware redeployment.

title: RL Agents Part 4: PPO, GRPO, and Search-Time Reasoning
summary: Understand PPO clipping, GRPO group-relative baselines, and MCTS-like reasoning search with runnable examples that connect training-time optimization and inference-time planning.

title: RL Agents Part 3: Outcome vs Process Rewards
summary: Learn the practical tradeoffs between outcome reward models and process reward models, with a clear view of why dense step-level signals are essential for long-horizon agent tasks.

title: RL Agents Part 2: RLVR Deep Dive
summary: A detailed walkthrough of verifiable rewards in math, code, SQL, and tool-use tasks, including verifier design principles, failure cases, and practical rollout considerations.

title: RL Agents Part 1: RLHF to RLVR Foundations
summary: A beginner-friendly practical walkthrough of the RLHF pipeline, its scaling limits for long-horizon agents, and the shift to RLVR with objective verifiable rewards in code, math, SQL, and tool-use tasks.

title: Detecting LLM Hallucinations: From Cheap to Accurate Methods
summary: A practical guide to six hallucination detection methods ranked from fastest and cheapest to most accurate, covering Internal State, Semantic Entropy, SelfCheckGPT, SAFE, FActScore, and LLM-as-Judge, with Python code for each and a final comparison table.

title: Python Concurrency Part 6: Embeddings, Orchestrator & End-to-End Walkthrough
summary: Complete the concurrent research agent with a ProcessPoolExecutor embedding agent for CPU-bound numpy work, a LangChain + Ollama orchestrator that coordinates all agents, and a step-by-step end-to-end walkthrough of every pipeline stage.

title: Python Concurrency Part 5: Memory Agent, Token Optimizer & Analysis Agent
summary: Build the threading-based SQLite memory agent with fire-and-forget state sync, a rolling LLM token optimizer that compresses conversation history, and the subprocess-based analysis agent with hard timeout enforcement and JSON pipe protocol.

title: Python Concurrency Part 4: Research Agent  Architecture & Async Fetch Agent
summary: Build a concurrent agentic research bot in Python. Part 4 covers the project architecture, core data models, session registry, and the asyncio-powered fetch agent that queries Wikipedia and HackerNews in parallel with asyncio.gather().

title: Python Concurrency Part 3: Threading  ThreadPoolExecutor & the GIL
summary: Master Python threading: understand the GIL, when threads beat async for blocking libraries, ThreadPoolExecutor, loop.run_in_executor(), thread-safe patterns with Lock and RLock, and thread-local storage  with real examples from the research agent memory layer.

title: Python Concurrency Part 2: Subprocess  Process Isolation & Async Pipes
summary: Learn when and how to use Python subprocesses: subprocess.run(), Popen(), asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(), the JSON stdin/stdout pipe protocol, hard timeouts with asyncio.wait_for(), and why isolation matters for agentic systems.

title: Python Concurrency Part 1: asyncio  Coroutines, Tasks & Synchronisation
summary: A complete asyncio guide: coroutines, the event loop, async/await, create_task, gather, TaskGroup, Futures, and all four synchronisation primitives (Lock, Semaphore, Event, Condition)  with runnable notebook examples throughout.

title: Google ADK Part 6: Extras  LiteLLM, Memory, GCP Deployment, Evals & More
summary: Go beyond the basics with Google ADK: connect Ollama and third-party LLMs via LiteLLM, manage session memory and state, deploy to GCP with Cloud Run or Vertex AI Agent Engine, evaluate model performance with adk eval, and explore callbacks, artifacts, structured output, and guardrails.

title: Google ADK Part 5: Parallel Agents  Concurrent Multi-Agent Execution
summary: Learn how to run multiple AI agents concurrently with Google ADK's ParallelAgent. Speed up workflows by executing independent sub-agents simultaneously and aggregating their results into a unified report.

title: Google ADK Part 4: Loop Agents  Iterative Processing with ADK
summary: Learn how to build iterative agent workflows with Google ADK's LoopAgent. Run agents in a loop, control max iterations, and signal early completion  perfect for content refinement, retries, and quality-gating.

title: Google ADK Part 3: Sequential Agents  Building Step-by-Step Pipelines
summary: Learn how to build deterministic agent pipelines with Google ADK's SequentialAgent. Chain multiple agents in a fixed order, pass structured data via shared state, and replace LLM routing with guaranteed step execution.

title: Google ADK Part 2: Multi-Agent Systems with Sub-Agents
summary: Learn how to build a multi-agent system with Google ADK. Create specialised sub-agents with their own tools and wire them into an orchestrator agent that delegates tasks automatically using Gemini.

title: Google ADK: Building AI Agents with Google's Agent Development Kit
summary: Get started with Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK): install it, learn the key CLI commands, build a sample agent, create a custom tool, and run your agent via terminal and the web UI.

title: Pricing Insights: Analyzing E-commerce Price Trends & Dynamic Pricing
summary: A deep dive into building a production-ready analytics pipeline for e-commerce price trends, dynamic pricing, and business intelligence using SQL, Python, and Plotly.

title: Hitlog Processing: Analyzing User Journeys to Identify Influential Content
summary: Build a production-ready Python application to analyze user journeys and identify which articles drive registrations. Explore timestamp-based and graph-based approaches with clean architecture.

title: NumPy Complete Guide: From Arrays to Advanced Computing
summary: Master NumPy from basics to advanced: arrays, operations, indexing, slicing, broadcasting, linear algebra, and performance optimization for numerical computing in Python.

title: Pandas Part 3: Advanced Data Engineering
summary: Master advanced Pandas techniques: data partitioning, chunking, memory optimization, parallel processing, vectorization, and production-ready best practices for data engineering.

title: Pandas Part 2: Joins and GroupBy Operations
summary: Master Pandas joins, merges, and GroupBy operations: inner/left/right/outer joins, multi-key merges, aggregations, transforms, pivot tables, and window functions.

title: Pandas Part 1: From Installation to Data Cleaning
summary: Master Pandas fundamentals: installation, data structures, reading/writing files, data cleaning, and filtering operations for effective data manipulation in Python.

title: Deep Dive: Functions in Our Hindi Wikipedia Scraper
summary: Have you ever wondered how to automatically retrieve and clean Hindi text from Wikipedia? This post walks through the requests and BeautifulSoup modules and explains each function in a reusable scraper class, ending with the full ready-to-run code.

title: GitHub Advanced: Actions, Workflows, and Open Source Contribution
summary: Master advanced Git and GitHub: GitHub Actions, CI/CD workflows, .gitignore, pre-commit hooks, contributing to open source, releases, tags, and GitHub Packages.

title: GitHub Basics: Mastering Version Control and Collaboration
summary: Master the fundamentals of Git and GitHub: version control, repositories, branches, commits, pull requests, staging area, and essential CLI commands for modern development.

title: LangGraph Agent to HTML Blog Generator: Building Blogs with AI
summary: Learn how to generate HTML blog posts from Jupyter notebooks using LangChain, LangGraph, and Ollama. This post walks through loading, retrieving, reasoning, and generating structured blog content with a full pipeline.

title: Graph-Powered RAG with LangChain, Chroma DB, and Gemma 3
summary: Build an advanced Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline using LangGraph and Gemma 3 model with detailed code and visualization.

title: Building a RAG Pipeline with PDFs, Chroma DB, and Gemma 3
summary: Step-by-step guide to building a Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline using LangChain, HuggingFace embeddings, and your own PDF (resume) as a knowledge base.

title: LangChain Document Loaders: A Unified Gateway to Your Data
summary: Explore how to use LangChain's powerful document loaders to bring in data from PDFs, websites, YouTube, CSVs, Notion, and even structured JSON with just a few lines of code.

title: LangChain + Ollama: Your Gateway to LLM Apps, Explained with Code
summary: This blog walks through how to build with LangChain and Ollama covering chat vs. completion models, prompt templates, and hands-on code to get started fast.

title: Setting Up Ollama
summary: Get started with Ollama, a no-token, local-friendly way to run LLMs on your machine. Setup steps and essential commands included.

title: Grid Traversal Algorithms: BFS vs DFS Explained with Code
summary: Learn how to implement and visualize Breadth-First Search (BFS) and Depth-First Search (DFS) on a 2D matrix. This beginner-friendly guide walks you through both algorithms with code, output, and a comparison table.

title: Binary Search Trees in Python: From Scratch
summary: Learn how to implement a Binary Search Tree (BST) from scratch in Python, covering insertion, search, deletion, traversal, and visualization.

title: Sorting Algorithms Demystified: Visualized and Benchmarked in Python
summary: Explore and compare Bubble, Insertion, Selection, Merge, and Quick Sort through Python code, visual benchmarks, and complexity analysis.

title: Exploring Search Algorithms: From Linear to Binary
summary: Learn the fundamentals of Linear and Binary Search algorithms, their implementation in Python, and when to use each. Includes step-by-step code and comparison.

title: Decision Tree Regressor from Scratch: Split, Predict, Repeat
summary: Explore how decision trees make predictions by learning to split data based on error reduction. This guide walks through a full tree regressor implementation in Python.

title: Lasso Regression from Scratch: Feature Selection Meets Regularization
summary: Learn how to implement Lasso Regression using Coordinate Descent from scratch, and explore how L1 regularization leads to feature selection in high-dimensional data.

title: Ridge Regression from Scratch: With Gradient Descent and L2 Regularization
summary: Explore how to implement Ridge Regression using gradient descent from scratch. Learn why regularization matters and compare performance against OLS and SGD models.

title: Mastering Linear Regression with OLS: A Hands-On Guide Using California Housing Data
summary: Dive deep into Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) linear regression with a real-world dataset. This blog walks you through each step from loading data to evaluating model performance.

title: Linear Regression from Scratch: With Gradient Descent
summary: This blog breaks down gradient descent like never before and walks you through building your own predictive model from scratch. Simple, sharp, and hands-on.

title: Ruff: The Blazingly Fast Python Linter and Formatter
summary: Master Ruff, the all-in-one Python linting and formatting tool that's 10-100x faster than traditional tools. Learn how to replace Flake8, Black, isort, and more with a single command.

title: Mastering Pytest: A Deep Dive into Decorators and Advanced Testing
summary: Unlock the full power of pytest by mastering its decorator system. Learn @pytest.mark.parametrize, @pytest.fixture, custom markers, and 10+ essential decorators with practical examples.

title: UV: The Lightning-Fast Python Package Manager
summary: Discover UV, the blazingly fast Python package manager built in Rust. Learn how it's 10-100x faster than pip and why it's becoming the go-to tool for modern Python development.

title: Understanding pyproject.toml: The Modern Python Project Configuration File
summary: Master pyproject.toml - the unified configuration standard for Python projects. Learn structure, benefits, tool configurations, migration from setup.py, and best practices.

title: Setting Up Your Python Environment
summary: A beginner-friendly guide to creating and managing Python virtual environments, installing packages, and using pip effectively.

title: Hello World! My First Blog Post
summary: Kicking off my blog with excitement and a glimpse of what's to come. Let the journey begin!
